records.ety: [F. extase, L. ecstasis, fr. Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to put out of place, derange; <?/ = 'ek out + <?/ to set, stand. See Ex-, and Stand.]

defs: The state of being beside one's self or rapt out of one's self; a state in which the mind is elevated above the reach of ordinary impressions, as when under the influence of overpowering emotion; an extraordinary elevation of the spirit, as when the soul, unconscious of sensible objects, is supposed to contemplate heavenly mysteries.
quotes: Like a mad prophet in an ecstasy.
This is the very ecstasy of love.

defs: Excessive and overmastering joy or enthusiasm; rapture; enthusiastic delight.
quotes: He on the tender grass Would sit, and hearken even to ecstasy.

defs: Violent distraction of mind; violent emotion; excessive grief of anxiety; insanity; madness.
quotes: That unmatched form and feature of blown youth Blasted with ecstasy.
Our words will but increase his ecstasy.

defs: A state which consists in total suspension of sensibility, of voluntary motion, and largely of mental power. The body is erect and inflexible; the pulsation and breathing are not affected.
To fill ecstasy, or with rapture or enthusiasm.
quotes: The most ecstasied order of holy . . . spirits.
